task: Fix Discord webhook pickup from local .env file and commit/push changes to askr repository
discussion_summary: Identified that the global ~/.config/askr/.env was taking precedence over local .env, preventing Discord webhook from being loaded. The fix involved updating env.py to use override=False on local dotenv load, and updating askr.py to call env.load() at startup. Changes were committed and pushed. User clarified that friend only needs to git pull — no need to rerun askr init since env.load() fires on every import.

accomplishments.what: Identified root cause: global .env override preventing local webhook pickup
Fixed env.py to use override=False on local .env load
Fixed askr.py to call env.load() at startup
Committed and pushed both changes to repository

next_actions.action: Inform friend to run 'git pull' in askr clone to get the env.py and askr.py fixes
next_actions.why: Changes are now pushed and ready to be pulled

next_actions.action: Verify friend's local .env has ASKR_DISCORD_WEBHOOK set correctly
next_actions.why: The fix now allows local .env to be read; webhook must exist in that file

next_actions.action: Have friend run any askr command (e.g., 'askr' or 'askr init') to trigger env.load() and pick up webhook
next_actions.why: env.load() runs on every import, so next command execution will load the webhook automatically

next_actions.action: Test that project brief is sent to Discord webhook on next askr session
next_actions.why: Confirms the webhook is now being picked up and used correctly

decisions.decision: No need to ask friend to rerun 'askr init' after git pull
decisions.reason: env.load() fires on every import, so the webhook will be picked up automatically on next command execution

relational_files.why: Local .env file now properly read for Discord webhook and other keys
Global env file that was previously overriding local .env; now respects local values
